title: Tech Disruptors: Cerebras After IPO: OpenAI, AWS and Inference
summary: “OpenAI has only two AI accelerator compute vendors in production today, Cerebras and Nvidia,” Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman says. Four days after Cerebras went public, Feldman joined Bloomberg Intelligence’s Kunjan Sobhani to discuss the company’s next chapter and the rapidly shifting AI infrastructure landscape. title: Vanguards of Healthcare: Ocular Therapeutix Cuts Blindness Toll
summary: “Forty percent of patients in this country alone in the first year drop out [of current retinal disease treatments] and every one of those patients ends up going blind. By far the most expensive disease in the world is blindness and the reason for that is really quite simple. It’s because the mortality doesn’t change, the use of resources skyrockets, but the mortality doesn’t change. summary: Source: Bloomberg, 14:48

title: Aluminum Market Tightness Could Continue for 'Long Time,' Morgan Stanley Says
summary: Amy Gower, metals and mining commodities strategist at Morgan Stanley, discusses the impact of the Iran war on aluminum prices. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, she warns the market could "stay quite tight" for a long time. "Most of the smelters that are shut are damaged, and so they're looking at sort of twelve-month restart timelines," Gower explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Petrobras Hikes Gasoline Prices After Brazil Subsidies Approved
summary: Brazil’s oil producer Petrobras is increasing domestic gasoline prices for distributors after the government approved new subsidies to shield consumers from Iran war-fueled crude price hikes.

title: Gold Advances as Report of US-Iran Deal Eases Higher Rates Bets
summary: Gold edged higher after Axios reported that Iran and the US have reached a deal to extend a truce and work toward an agreement to end the war, pending final approval from US President Donald Trump.

title: Earnings are Trumping Everything, Says Bob Doll
summary:
Earnings estimates are surging and the growth of AI spending has served as a counterweight to the hit from Iran.
Bob Doll, CIO at Crossmark Global Investments, joins for a discussion on equity resiliency and warning from the bond vigilantes. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks and Bonds Rise After US-Iran Deal Report: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders sent stocks toward all-time highs, bonds rose while a rally in oil waned on speculation the US and Iran are close to a diplomatic breakthrough in the war that has rattled global markets.

title: MoneyGram’s Big Crypto Bet
summary: MoneyGram CEO Anthony Soohoo joins Dani Burger on Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about why cash still dominates globally despite the crypto boom. He reveals how MoneyGram’s 500,000 retail locations give it a unique edge and discusses the impact of US immigration crackdowns on remittances. Plus, why the company’s Kraken partnership could reshape crypto spending worldwide. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Stocks Reverse Losses on Iran Deal Report as Oil Pares Gains
summary: Stocks rebounded from earlier losses on Thursday, as investors reacted to a reported deal between the US and Iran.

title: Why Markets Are Ignoring Bad News
summary: Ardian Co-CEO Mark Benedetti joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why markets keep hitting record highs despite wars, inflation, and rate uncertainty. He breaks down the massive shift that's happening inside private markets, why diversification is making a comeback, and how the booming secondary market could become a $300 billion industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Pares Gain on News of Possible US-Iran Pact to Extend Truce
summary: Oil pared gains on a report that the US and Iran have reached a preliminary agreement to extend the ceasefire between the two countries and launch negotiations on the Islamic Republic’s nuclear program.

title: Citadel Securities Sees Markets Underpricing Signs of Iran Deal
summary: Investors may be underestimating the chances of a “timely” reopening of the Strait of Hormuz triggering a broad relief rally across financial markets, according to Citadel Securities.

title: Copasa Relaunches Share Offering After Bids Came Below Minimum
summary: Cia de Saneamento de Minas Gerais announced new terms for its privatization process, after two bids for a 30% stake in the Brazilian water utility came in below the government’s expectations.

title: Dollar Falls Against Major Peers on Report of US-Iran Deal
summary: The dollar fell against all of its major peers after a report that the US and Iran reached an agreement to extend a ceasefire, pending the approval of President Donald Trump.

title: Argentine Provinces Tap Global Markets That Sovereign Avoids
summary: Argentine provinces are tapping global bond markets at the fastest pace in nearly a decade, even as the federal government sticks to short-term debt in the local market amid investor fears over the 2027 elections.

title: US and Iran Reach Deal to Extend Truce, Axios Reports
summary: Iran and the US have reached a deal to extend a truce and work toward an agreement to end the war, Axios reported, sending oil prices lower. Tyler Kendall reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasuries Rise After Reported US-Iran Deal to Extend Truce
summary: Treasuries rose after a report that the US and Iran reached an agreement to extend a truce and work toward an end to a three-month war that’s ignited an oil-driven surge in inflation.

title: Avis’ Shock Split With Vendor Revives Credit’s Big Software Fear
summary: It in many ways embodies investors’ worst fears of how a SaaSpocalypse might play out.

title: Odd Lots Is Coming to Hong Kong
summary: Come test your wits.

title: China Sells $885 Million of Green Bonds in Hong Kong Debut
summary: China priced 6 billion yuan ($885 million) of green sovereign bonds on Thursday, a year after the government’s debut in the international ESG debt market.

title: Drone Stocks Soar on News of Trump Team Eyeing Funding Deals
summary: Shares of drone makers and suppliers rallied Thursday after a report said the Trump Administration is exploring potential funding deals with a group of companies to increase domestic production and lower costs.

title: Drone Makers Quantum and Destinus Seek Banks for 2027 IPOs
summary: Quantum Systems and Destinus are in talks to line up advisers for potential IPOs as soon as early 2027, people familiar with the matter said, as the drone makers seek to tap surging demand for unmanned defense assets.

title: Goldman's Snider Sees Earnings Driving Stocks Higher
summary: Goldman Sachs Chief US Equity Strategist Ben Snider discusses the recent increase in the firm's S&P 500 price target, highlighting strong earnings growth as the primary driver behind the adjustment. Speaking with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Snider also says he expects AI gains to broaden out in the market. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: EU’s Economic Powers Seek Unity to Push Capital Markets Merger
summary: The European Union’s six largest economies are advancing plans for a bloc-wide capital markets union, hoping their collective voice will spur the continent to action after years of delay.

title: Bitcoin Falls Below $73,000 Despite Trump’s Crypto Capital Push
summary: Bitcoin briefly fell below $73,000 despite a fresh push from Donald Trump for the CLARITY Act, suggesting pro-crypto messaging from  Washington is losing its ability to drive gains in digital assets.

title: Fed’s Williams Says Monetary Policy Well-Positioned Right Now
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams said the central bank’s policy is well-positioned to respond to the economic effects of the US war in Iran.

title: German Power Prices Swing From Zero to Almost €400 in a Day
summary: German power prices will swing sharply on Thursday, with electricity trading close to zero at noon before surging to nearly €400 ($465) a megawatt-hour in the evening as solar generation fades.

title: Fed's Jefferson Warns Inflation Risks Are Tilted to the Upside
summary: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson says he expects inflation to cool later this year as the effects of tariffs and higher energy costs wear off but cautions that inflationary risks remain tilted to the upside. He speaks at a conference hosted by the Bank of Japan in Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Russia Selling Yuan Bonds After Putin’s Visit to Beijing
summary: Russia is selling its second issue of yuan-denominated sovereign bonds after President Vladimir Putin held talks in Beijing last week.

title: Bank of Canada Says Markets More Vulnerable to Sharp Correction
summary: The Bank of Canada said the financial system has functioned well through recent global shocks, but highlighted the risk of an asset price correction as well as vulnerabilities related to the role hedge funds are playing in debt markets.

title: Oil Tankers Suffer Drone Attacks Off Turkey’s Black Sea Coast
summary: Three crude-oil tankers sanctioned for being part of Russia’s shadow fleet were attacked by drones off Turkey’s Black Sea coast overnight, according to a local port agent.
